nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Molle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cecile, Emile, Jacques, Sylvain.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of mol, mou ‘soft’, denoting boggy ground.

variants.name: Mollet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jacques, Jean Claude.
French and Swiss French: topographic name for someone who lived in a muddy or boggy place, from a diminutive of mol ‘marsh, bog’ (compare Molle ). This surname is also found in Germany.

variants.name: Mole
meanings: French: metonymic occupational name for a knife grinder or a maker of whetstones, from a variant of meule ‘whetstone, grindstone, millstone’.

en.lifeSummary: de Molle was born about 1040, in Dunbar, East Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 daughter.
